label: Russian artillery/missile strikes on Kharkiv
damage: Multiple residential areas and civilian infrastructure destroyed
casualties: Hundreds of civilians killed in Kharkiv Oblast during 2022
notes: Russia struck Ukraine's 2nd-largest city relentlessly from Belgorod Oblast from the first day of the invasion

label: Kalibr cruise missiles from Crimea to Kyiv
damage: Energy infrastructure, power stations, and civilian areas hit across Ukraine
casualties: 19 civilians killed, 105 injured in Oct 10 mass strike
notes: Oct 10 2022 mass missile strike was Russia's response to Kerch Bridge explosion; targeted power grid across multiple cities

label: Shahed-136 drone swarm toward Kyiv
damage: Several buildings damaged in Kyiv despite high interception rate
notes: Ukraine's air defense achieved 96% interception rate on this date; Shahed drones typically approach from multiple directions

label: Russian cruise missiles and artillery on Kherson city
damage: Market, hospital, and residential areas hit
casualties: 78 civilians killed in Kherson city in first 6 months after liberation (Nov 2022-May 2023)
notes: Russia shelled Ukrainian-controlled Kherson city daily from left (eastern) bank of Dnipro after November 2022 withdrawal

label: Russian Grad/BM-21 rocket attacks on Sumy Oblast
damage: Civilian infrastructure in Sumy Oblast destroyed
casualties: Multiple civilian deaths in cross-border shelling
notes: Sumy Oblast experienced sustained cross-border shelling throughout the war from Belgorod and Kursk oblasts

label: Russian artillery barrage on Bakhmut
damage: Systematic destruction of all buildings in Bakhmut; city 90%+ destroyed
casualties: Thousands of civilians trapped; hundreds killed over 10-month battle
notes: Bakhmut sustained the highest concentration of Russian artillery fire of the war; Russia used 50,000+ shells per day at peak

label: Russian Black Sea Fleet missile strike on Odesa
damage: Military infrastructure and residential buildings hit
notes: Russia struck Odesa the day after the sinking of the Moskva cruiser as retaliation

label: Russian advance and shelling on Chernihiv
damage: Chernihiv cut off; bridges destroyed; civilian areas shelled
casualties: Hundreds of civilians killed during siege
notes: Chernihiv was encircled for 30 days; Ukrainian defenders held out until Russian withdrawal in March 2022

label: Neptune missile strike sinks Moskva cruiser
damage: Russian Black Sea Fleet flagship Moskva sunk — largest warship destroyed in combat since Falklands War
casualties: Russia admits 1 KIA, 27 missing; actual losses higher per US intelligence
notes: Ukraine used decoy Bayraktar TB2 drone to distract Moskva's radar, then struck with two R-360 Neptune anti-ship missiles

label: Kerch Bridge explosion (Ukrainian operation)
damage: Partial collapse of road section; railway damaged; fuel tanker fire
notes: Explosion on Putin's 70th birthday; attributed to Ukrainian truck bomb or sea drone; disrupted the Crimea Land Bridge supply route for Russian forces

label: Ukrainian ATACMS strike on Belgorod military targets
damage: Military depot and logistic hub damaged
notes: First use of ATACMS against Russian territory after Biden authorized deep strikes in November 2024

label: Ukrainian naval drone swarm on Sevastopol
damage: Minesweeper Pyatidyesyatiyetiye Pobedy damaged; Black Sea Fleet HQ hit
notes: Ukraine's naval drone campaign steadily degraded Russia's Black Sea Fleet; Russia eventually moved fleet away from Sevastopol to Novorossiysk

label: Ukraine's Kursk Incursion: Cross-border advance
damage: 1,000+ sq km of Russian territory captured; Sudzha town taken
casualties: Russian and DPRK military casualties; Ukrainian forces also suffered losses
notes: First foreign military occupation of Russian territory since WWII; Ukraine used combined arms — armored vehicles, drones, artillery — to advance rapidly

label: 2014-2022 Minsk Contact Line
notes: The line of control established by Minsk II (Feb 2015) remained largely unchanged until February 2022; represented frozen conflict with 14,000 deaths

label: Russian axis of advance: Belarus → Chernobyl → Kyiv
notes: Russian forces advanced from Belarus through the Chernobyl exclusion zone to reach Kyiv's northern suburbs within 24 hours; 40-mile convoy stalled

label: Crimea to Zaporizhzhia: Russian land corridor aspiration
notes: Russia established a land corridor from Crimea through Kherson Oblast to Zaporizhzhia as early as March 2022; fully captured Kherson Oblast before liberating it in November 2022

label: Ukrainian Kharkiv counteroffensive axis (Sept 2022)
notes: Ukrainian forces thrust from Kharkiv Oblast south through Izium, liberating 8,000 sq km in days; Russia abandoned Izium logistics hub and withdrew east of Oskil River

label: Black Sea Grain Initiative corridor (July 2022–July 2023)
notes: UN-brokered corridor allowed Ukrainian grain exports via Black Sea; 33 million metric tons exported before Russia exited July 2023; prevented global food price spike

label: Weapons and Aid Transit: Western Ukraine Corridor
notes: Western military aid flows into Ukraine through western border crossings at Uzhhorod, Lviv, and other points; primary supply line for Western weapons systems

label: Pereiaslav Agreement: Ukraine–Muscovy alignment (1654)
notes: The 1654 Pereiaslav Agreement represented the formal alignment (disputed in nature) of Cossack Ukraine with Muscovy — a pivotal moment in Russian imperial claims over Ukraine. This symbolic line from Pereiaslav to Moscow represents the diplomatic connection.

label: Active Donbas front line (approximate, 2024)
notes: The Donetsk front line stabilized through 2024 with Russian advances south of Avdiivka and toward Pokrovsk; Ukrainian forces in defensive posture

label: Russia's first Oreshnik ballistic missile strike on Dnipro
damage: Pivdenmash industrial facility damaged; unprecedented missile used in combat
notes: First combat use of the Oreshnik medium-range ballistic missile; hypersonic warhead; Russia claimed it cannot be intercepted by existing systems; served as nuclear signaling after ATACMS authorization

label: Zaporizhzhia front line (2023 counteroffensive axis)
notes: Ukraine's 2023 summer counteroffensive targeted the Zaporizhzhia-Melitopol axis; despite months of fighting, breakthrough limited to ~17km; Russian defensive lines proved among the most fortified since WWII

label: German forces in Kyiv: Babi Yar massacre route (1941)
notes: German forces organized the mass deportation of Kyiv's Jewish population to Babi Yar ravine on September 29-30, 1941, murdering 33,771 people in two days

label: Istanbul peace talks (March 29, 2022)
notes: Ukraine and Russia held peace talks in Istanbul (March 29, 2022) following Russian withdrawal from Kyiv. Talks produced a preliminary framework; collapsed after Bucha massacre was revealed. The closest the sides came to a negotiated settlement.
